title: cistus × wintonensis
intro: Cistus × wintonensis, also known as Winton Rockrose, is a hybrid plant species within the Cistus genus, characterized by its showy, white flowers and drought-tolerant properties. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of Cistus × wintonensis include its potential to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, Cistus × wintonensis is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, or added to topical creams and ointments for its medicinal properties.
